# Transportation Brief: Staff
**Post-decision community lens**

Canonical overview: [Post-Decision Transportation Brief](../POST-DECISION-BRIEF.md)

## What has been decided

The board has approved the transportation-relevant parts of the reconfiguration. Staff now have to turn the decision into arrival, dismissal, and coverage routines that work in real buildings.

## What this means for you

Teachers, administrators, and support staff will need clear handoffs around bus unloading, first-period supervision, dismissal order, and communications with families. The work is operational, not theoretical.

## What still needs to be worked out

- Final bell times for each building
- How the district covers the driver shortfall
- How arrival and dismissal duties shift when buildings change grade bands
- What staff need to tell families, and when
- How special cases are routed and supervised

## What is confirmed, what is estimated

Confirmed: 20 drivers were confirmed at the vote, and the district is already in implementation mode.

Estimated: the approved plan is modeled to need about 30 drivers, leaving a 10-13 driver gap; route-expansion cost is in the hundreds of thousands, and building teams face a broad daily coordination burden.

The canonical brief has the shared facts and the decision context.
